threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's a 'charming stroll' to the high street, not a sprint to catch the last bus after dark.
Caught a late 61 last night, driver had the best playlist. It’s weird how the city feels more alive at 3 AM than any other time.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I saw a kid selling homemade lemonade in front of the Späti, while a group of older men debated the latest Bauprojekte. Classic Neukölln vibes.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a random street party that turns into an open-air rave, complete with local DJs spinning on balconies at 3 AM.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know not to blink at the noise from the taco trucks at 2 AM, while newcomers clutch their phones like shields.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy in a full tuxedo getting off the Muni at 5 AM. He tipped his hat to the driver as he strolled off like it was prime time.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm is in the little plazas where locals gossip over café con leche.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they fumble with the metro map, still trying to figure out the L2 stops between Sagrada Família and La Pau.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot a newcomer when they still think Dalston Junction is the main event and not the many hidden bars around.
I swear I saw a guy on the N133 who’d brought a whole Monopoly board, challenging everyone to play. He lost to a drunk lady in under ten minutes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a wild cat sunbathing in the middle of my street, with locals just stepping around it like it was totally normal.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a currywurst stand in a former public toilet, serving up greasy nostalgia right next to an artisanal coffee shop.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can instantly name the best taco truck by the park, not just the shiny new spot on 24th.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy serenading the 19 bus passengers with a ukulele. Everyone seemed kinda annoyed, but you could tell it lightened the mood.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always think the plazas are perfect. But locals know they’re just waiting for the next party to mess up the benches again.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their coffee order—still asking for 'Americanos' instead of a simple 'café'.
